aou_req__log__mitigate_unbounded_init.title: Mitigate Unbounded Initialization

aou_req__log__no_console_in_production.content: Console Logging shall not be used in production.
aou_req__log__no_console_in_production.title: No Console Logging in Production

aou_req__log__no_private_api.title: No Private API Usage

aou_req__log__no_safety_reliance_on_log.title: No Safety Reliance on Log Output

aou_req__log__no_static_storage_use.title: No Static Storage Usage

aou_req__log__no_unsupported_contexts.title: No Unsupported Context Usage

comp__data_router.content: This is the datarouter component responsible for routing log messages to remote Diagnostics Log and Trace (DLT) backend.
comp__data_router.title: Data Router

comp__mw_logging.content: This is the logging component library responsible for selecting the appropriate log sinks based on configuration at runtime. It can perform tasks such as log formatting, filtering, and composite backend selection based on runtime context and configuration. The logging component is designed to be extensible, allowing for custom logging backend to be added as needed.
comp__mw_logging.title: Logging Component

comp_req__data_router__dlt_bw_quota_conf.content:
The component shall have the DLT bandwidth quota configuration in the file located at
``./etc/log-channels.json`` relative to the application specific location ``/opt/``.
comp_req__data_router__dlt_bw_quota_conf.title: DLT Bandwidth Quota Configuration

comp_req__data_router__dlt_message_filte.content:
DLT server shall support filtering of messages within each channel. The filter shall be
parametrized with application ID, context ID, log level threshold. Initial filter set shall
be part of channel configuration file, located in the following path:
``<data_router binary location>/../etc/log-channels.json``.
comp_req__data_router__dlt_message_filte.title: DLT Message Filtering

comp_req__data_router__dlt_multiple_chan.content:
DLT server shall support multiple log channels as specified in AUTOSAR Diagnostic, Log and
Trace Protocol Specification. The following parameters shall be configurable statically via
a deployment-specific configuration file
(``<data_router binary location>/./etc/log-channels.json``): Number and names of channels,
Network configuration for channel output, Log level threshold per channel, Log channel
assignments per application/context.
comp_req__data_router__dlt_multiple_chan.title: DLT Multiple Channel Support

comp_req__data_router__dlt_quota_bw_redu.content:
It shall be possible to configure the action to drop DLT messages when a quota is exceeded.

Note: The configuration shall be provided as an attribute as part of the DLT quota
configuration file (:need:`comp_req__data_router__dlt_bw_quota_config`).
comp_req__data_router__dlt_quota_bw_redu.title: DLT Quota Bandwidth Reduction

comp_req__data_router__dlt_server.content:
Datarouter shall implement DLT server component. Datarouter provides the functionality to
send messages using DLT protocol over UDP multicast transport.
comp_req__data_router__dlt_server.title: Datarouter DLT Server

comp_req__data_router__dlt_verbose_messa.content:
DLT server shall provide support for verbose DLT messages. Messages sent from mw::log
library shall be sent via DLT using verbose mode.
comp_req__data_router__dlt_verbose_messa.title: DLT Verbose Messages

comp_req__log__autosar_log_trace_spec.content:
mw::log shall implement "Specification of Log and Trace for Adaptive Platform". Release of
specification used shall be in agreement with the rest of Adaptive AUTOSAR stack.
comp_req__log__autosar_log_trace_spec.title: AUTOSAR Log and Trace Specification

comp_req__log__avoid_locks.content:
mw::log shall be free of time-unbound locks and shall implement strategies to limit wait
time. Time limit shall be defined based on requirements from functions on individual ECU.
Mutual exclusion mechanisms shall include priority inversion protection.
comp_req__log__avoid_locks.title: Avoid Locks

comp_req__log__avoid_signal_processing.content: The component shall not register any signal handler.
comp_req__log__avoid_signal_processing.title: Avoid Signal Processing

comp_req__log__config_fallback_defaults.content:
mw::log shall fall back to a default value for a configuration item that is not available
from the configuration files.
comp_req__log__config_fallback_defaults.title: Configuration Fallback Defaults

comp_req__log__cross_locking.content:
Cross-application and cross-thread dependencies shall be avoided. A thread logging in a loop
shall not block other thread's execution.
comp_req__log__cross_locking.title: Cross-Locking Prevention

comp_req__log__discard_invalid_settings.content: mw::log shall discard invalid configuration settings.
comp_req__log__discard_invalid_settings.title: Discard Invalid Settings

comp_req__log__discard_missing_config.content: mw::log shall discard configuration files that are not found.
comp_req__log__discard_missing_config.title: Discard Missing Configuration Files

comp_req__log__discard_syntax_errors.content: mw::log shall discard configuration files with syntax errors.
comp_req__log__discard_syntax_errors.title: Discard Syntax Error Configuration

comp_req__log__dlt_verbose_mode.content:
mw::log shall implement DLT verbose mode. Generation of messages shall be done in
DLT-conform format, allowing messages to be sent by Datarouter without additional
reprocessing.
comp_req__log__dlt_verbose_mode.title: DLT Verbose Mode

comp_req__log__file_descriptor_flags.content:
The component shall set the ``FD_CLOEXEC`` (or ``O_CLOEXEC``) flag on all the file
descriptors it owns.

Note: This requirement can be satisfied in unit-test, by checking
``int flags = fcntl(fd, F_GETFD); assert((flags != -1) && (flags & FD_CLOEXEC));`` for every
file descriptor owned by the component.
comp_req__log__file_descriptor_flags.title: File Descriptor Flags

comp_req__log__forward_to_system_logger.content:
The system logger backend shall forward the logs to the native system logger mechanism.

Note: Under QNX, slogger2 shall be used.
comp_req__log__forward_to_system_logger.title: Forward to System Logger

comp_req__log__global_config_basis.content:
Global configuration shall be used as basis for all applications. Global configuration shall
remain in the file ``/etc/ecu_logging_config.json``. Application-specific configuration
shall override the global defaults.
comp_req__log__global_config_basis.title: Global Configuration Basis

comp_req__log__inactive_logstream.content:
mw::log library shall not perform any useful activity if log level of LogStream object is
insufficient for given context. LogStream object shall not generate serialized messages if
the severity of the message does not allow it to be sent.
comp_req__log__inactive_logstream.title: Inactive LogStream Behavior

comp_req__log__index_size_checking.content:
Indices in data structures and sizes of data accessed or copied shall be checked for
plausibility to avoid high runtime utilization through long iterations or data corruption.
comp_req__log__index_size_checking.title: Index and Size Checking

comp_req__log__local_allocation_strategy.content:
mw::log shall use local allocators to avoid using global heap. Global heap allocation (if
any) shall be limited to initialization phase of application lifecycle.
comp_req__log__local_allocation_strategy.title: Local Allocation Strategy

comp_req__log__log_config_parameters.content:
Application log configuration shall provide information necessary for log message generation
and filtering. The provided parameter set includes, but is not limited to the following:
application ID (appId), log mode for mw::log, default log level, log level assignments for
individual context IDs.
comp_req__log__log_config_parameters.title: Log Configuration Parameters

comp_req__log__memory_bound_checking.content: Memory boundaries shall be explicitly checked when writing to shared memory.
comp_req__log__memory_bound_checking.title: Memory Bound Checking

comp_req__log__no_endless_loops.content: mw::log shall not contain unbound loops or loops with unchecked exit conditions.
comp_req__log__no_endless_loops.title: No Endless Loops

comp_req__log__send_to_datarouter.content:
mw::log shall use logging library to send verbose messages to datarouter. Applications shall
use mw::log interface to log verbose mode messages. Internally mw::log shall be using logging
library.
comp_req__log__send_to_datarouter.title: Send Messages to Datarouter

comp_req__log__shm_file_permissions.content:
Logging shared-memory files shall have read-only posix file permission for group and others.
The shared-memory file created by mw::log shall be read-only for group and others.

Note: mw::log creates a shared memory file for each app.
comp_req__log__shm_file_permissions.title: Shared Memory File Permissions

comp_req__log__system_backend_activation.content: The system logger backend shall be enabled if and only if the log mode contains "kSystem".
comp_req__log__system_backend_activation.title: System Backend Activation

comp_req__log__uniform_config_file.content:
mw::log shall be uniformly configured via an application-specific configuration file.
Configuration file location shall be relative to application binary.
E.g.: ``./<application name>/etc/logging.json``.
comp_req__log__uniform_config_file.title: Uniform Configuration File

comp_req__log__use_log_trace_framework.content:
All output from logging and instrumentation based logging shall go through "mw::log"
framework.
comp_req__log__use_log_trace_framework.title: Use Log and Trace Framework

dec_rec__log__explicit_init.content:
Provide a library for explicit initialization of logging.

**Context:**

Currently there are multiple approaches to logging initialization.
Rust frontend configuration is provided as code and requires explicit initialization before any logging starts.
C++ configuration is lazily loaded on first use and based on a JSON file with path provided using environment variable.

This approach discrepancy makes it difficult to manage logging in multi-language environment.
Issue can be solved by providing an explicit initialization library.

Such explicit logging initialization shall:

- Initialize logging for Rust and C++ environments.
- Handle configuration of all sub-components of the logging environment.
- Provide interfaces to Rust and C++.

Examples of use:

- Initialize logging in Rust application:
  - Rust logging frontend
  - C++-based backend
  - Rust/C++ log bridge
- Initialize logging in C++ application utilizing Rust-based libraries:
  - C++ logging
  - Rust logging frontend utilized by libraries
  - C++-based backend
  - Rust/C++ log bridge

**Consequences**

Such change will require design changes to middleware logging.

In Rust it is a common practice to explicitly initialize logging before any logging starts.
This is true to both `score_log` logging frontend and ubiquitous `log` crate that is a defacto standard for logging in Rust.

Middleware logging library is currently initialized lazily on first use, using JSON config file.
It is not possible to perform the initialization operation explicitly.

**Justification for the Decision**

Explicit logging initialization will simplify logging initialization in multi-language environments.
dec_rec__log__explicit_init.title: Explicit initialization of logging

mod__logging_repo.content: The logging module provides a standardized logging framework for C++ and Rust projects using Bazel build system. It includes components for log routing allowing for flexible log management and integration with various logging backends. The module is designed to be extensible and configurable to meet the needs of different applications and environments.
mod__logging_repo.title: Logging

stkh_req__docgen_enabled__example.rationale: Ensure documentation builds are possible for all modules
stkh_req__docgen_enabled__example.title: Example Functional Requirement

arch.description: Mapping of uml key to uml content.

constraints.description: List of constraint names, which are defined for this need.

constraints_error.description: An error message set if any constraint failed, and `error_message` field is set in config.

constraints_passed.description: True if all constraints passed, False if any failed, None if not yet checked.

constraints_results.description: Mapping of constraint name, to check name, to result, None if not yet checked.

content.description: The main content of the need.

docname.description: Name of the document where the need is defined (None if external).

doctype.description: The markup type of the content, denoted by the suffix of the source file, e.g. '.rst'.

external_css.description: CSS class name, added to the external reference.

external_url.description: URL of the need, if it is an external need.

has_dead_links.description: True if any links reference need ids that are not found in the need list.

has_forbidden_dead_links.description: True if any links reference need ids that are not found in the need list, and the link type does not allow dead links.

is_external.description: If true, no node is created and need is referencing external url.

is_import.description: If true, the need was derived from an import.

is_modified.description: Whether the need was modified by needextend.

jinja_content.description: Whether the content was pre-processed by jinja.

layout.description: Key of the layout, which is used to render the need.

lineno.description: Line number where the need is defined (None if external).

parts.description: Mapping of parts, a.k.a. sub-needs, IDs to data that overrides the need's data

post_template.description: The template key, if the post_content was created from a jinja template.

pre_template.description: The template key, if the pre_content was created from a jinja template.

signature.description: Derived from a docutils desc_name node.

style.description: Comma-separated list of CSS classes (all appended by `needs_style_`).

template.description: The template key, if the content was created from a jinja template.
